title: Giannis Antetokounmpo paid high praise to Celtics’ coach Joe Mazzulla
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla for his strong mentality and leadership despite team challenges.
Mazzulla led the Celtics to the second seed in the East without key player Jayson Tatum for much of the season.
Antetokounmpo highlighted the difference in team culture compared to his experience with the Milwaukee Bucks this season.

title: Person seriously injured in fall at Firefighting Academy facility
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A person was seriously injured in a fall at the Firefighting Academy facility in Stow on Tuesday morning.
Emergency crews provided medical care and requested two additional ambulances at the scene.
Due to weather, the patient was transported by ground to UMass Memorial Medical Center instead of by helicopter.

title: TravelMag went on a seafood road trip in Maine and named these ‘can’t-miss’ restaurants. Which is your favorite?
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: TravelMag named several 'can't-miss' seafood restaurants during a road trip along Maine's coast.
Highlighted restaurants include The Clam Shack in Kennebunkport and Bagaduce Lunch in Penobscot.
Readers are invited to share their favorite coastal Maine restaurants with Boston.com editorial staff.

title: Ex-Stoughton cop Matthew Farwell’s DNA was on strap used to strangle Sandra Birchmore, prosecutors say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: DNA from former police officer Matthew Farwell was found on the strap used to strangle Sandra Birchmore.
Matthew Farwell is accused of killing Birchmore in 2021, believing he was the father of her unborn child.
Birchmore's death was initially ruled a suicide, but Farwell was indicted in 2024 after federal investigation.

title: A young mother rescued her 2-year-old son after their car crashed into a Vermont river
author: John Waller
bullets: A 26-year-old mother rescued her 2-year-old son after their car crashed into the Ottauquechee River in Vermont.
The mother unbuckled her son from a car seat, cut through an airbag, and swam to safety.
Both were taken to Dartmouth Hitchcock Medical Center with no apparent serious injuries; the crash is under investigation.

title: All the places you can go on the Amtrak Downeaster
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Amtrak Downeaster celebrates 25 years with service from Boston to Brunswick covering 12 stations across 143 miles.
Passengers enjoy amenities like free Wi-Fi, electrical outlets, and the Downeaster Cafe on board each train.
Value fares range from $3 to $36; seniors over 65 receive 50% off without needing a promo code.

title: ‘Boston College is about to have a renaissance in basketball’: Dan Hurley’s take on Luke Murray
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Luke Murray appointed head coach of Boston College basketball after leaving UConn, following Earl Grant's firing.
Dan Hurley praises Luke Murray as Boston College's new coach, predicting a basketball renaissance for the team.
Murray, formerly an assistant at UConn, aims to revitalize a team with only two winning seasons since 2011.

title: MBTA sets $80 round-trip train fare for World Cup games
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MBTA sets an $80 round-trip fare for trains between South Station and Gillette Stadium for World Cup games in 2026.
Tickets available April 8 on mTicket app and valid for travel across the Commuter Rail network on match days.
MBTA to operate 14 express trains on Franklin/Foxboro Line with no regular subway or bus service to Gillette Stadium.

title: New on Netflix and more: Top streaming picks for April 2026
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix releases '28 Years Later: The Bone Temple,' a zombie thriller featuring Ralph Fiennes experimenting with a virus cure.
Classic movies like 'The Truman Show' and 'The Devil Wears Prada' are now streaming on Paramount+ and HBO Max, respectively.
Zendaya and Robert Pattinson star in new Boston-filmed dark comedy 'The Drama,' receiving mixed reviews from Boston.com and the Globe.

title: Details emerge on fatal police shooting of 27-year-old woman in N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Megan Whiting, 27, was shot by police after allegedly attacking a resident with a fire extinguisher in Northfield, NH.
Officers found Whiting armed with a knife near an injured woman before an officer fired, resulting in her death.
Authorities are withholding the officer's identity and body camera footage pending further investigation into the incident.
